TranslationContext: This file contains Unit Names for the Dead Nations Faction.

Translations.Comment: Direct translation. Мертвые (Dead) + Государства (Nations). Evokes the undead kingdoms rising from ancient graves.
Скелет (skeleton) + Воины (warriors). Basic undead infantry. Hyphenated for compound clarity.
Зомби (zombie) - loanword universally recognized in Russian. Shambling undead hordes.
Бармалей (wight/spectre) is an archaic Russian term evoking ancient evil. Стража (guard) conveys elite warriors. Alternative: Привидение could be used but is too modern.
Копьеносцы (spear-bearers) + Скелеты (skeletons). Emphasizes disciplined undead formations with classical terminology.
Вырытая (unearthed/excavated) + Катапульта (catapult). Ancient siege weapon raised from burial grounds.
Лучники (archers) + Скелеты (skeletons). Undead archers - silent and deadly. Consistent naming pattern with other skeleton units.
Теневые (shadow) Волки (wolves) conveys supernatural, cursed nature. Alternative: Грозные Волки (dread wolves) for more menace.
Рыцари (knights) + Скелеты (skeletons). Undead cavalry - former knights serving in death. Hyphenated for compound clarity.
Страх (dread/fear) + Рыцари (knights). Elite undead knights radiating terror and supernatural menace.
Ужасные (fell/dreadful) + Летучие мыши (bats). Monstrous flying creatures of darkness. Alternative: Мрачные летучие мыши (grim bats).
Зомби (zombie) + Великан (giant). Hyphenated compound. Massive reanimated corpse of immense power.
Костяной (bone/osseous) + Дракон (dragon). Direct compound. Terrifying undead dragon of pure bone and malice.
Властелин (lord/master) + Привидение (wight/spectre). Ancient undead noble commanding the armies of the dead. Hyphenated for mythic weight.
Рыцарь (knight) + Вампир (vampire). Hyphenated compound. Undead knight blessed with vampiric powers and immortal hunger.
Некромант (necromancer) - direct loanword used in Russian fantasy literature. Master of death magic commanding undead legions.
